commonProblems.h2: Common Citroen Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Citroen’s diesel range, particularly the DV6 (1.6 HDi) and BlueHDi engines, dominate replacement searches in the UK due to commonality, while the 1.2 PureTech petrol presents a different, timing-belt driven failure pattern. Across the board, failures typically stem from emissions equipment clogging, oil system design, or timing component fatigue. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point — plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the specific failures affecting popular Citroen models and what it truly costs to fix them, comparing dealer versus specialist routes so you can decide whether repair or replacement is the right call.

problems.h4: 1.2 PureTech Wet Belt Degradation – The Oil Contamination Failure
problems.affectedModels: Citroen C3, C4, C4 Cactus, C5 Aircross (2014–2022, EB2/EB2DT engines)
problems.rootCause: The engine uses a rubber timing belt running inside the engine oil. Over time, the belt degrades, shedding rubber particles that block the oil strainer, leading to oil starvation and catastrophic bottom-end failure.
problems.recommendation: If you have caught the belt at a routine service before any oil pressure warnings, pay a qualified specialist to perform the full belt change, sump drop, and oil strainer cleaning. The dealer’s fixed-price belt change quote often does NOT include sump cleaning . If you have seen a low oil pressure warning, even briefly, the engine is compromised. Do not throw money at repairs – arrange a rebuilt engine through our network, specifying the revised belt design.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt 1.2 PureTech engine prices for your C3 or C4

problems.h4: DV6 / 1.6 HDi EGR & DPF Clogging – The Short Trip Killer
problems.affectedModels: Citroen Berlingo, C3, C4, C5, DS3, Relay (2008–2018, DV6ATED4, DV6C, DV6D engines)
problems.rootCause: This engine’s emissions system (EGR valve and DPF) clogs severely on vehicles used predominantly for short journeys or low-speed driving. The engine management system limits performance and forces DPF regeneration attempts, which fail on short trips.
problems.recommendation: If your Berlingo or C4 Picasso is worth under £3,000, replacing the DPF alone will likely exceed the car’s value. For high-mileage diesel cars used for short runs, consider selling as a non-runner or fitting a rebuilt engine through EngineMarket – the cost is comparable to major emissions repairs but gives you a fresh engine with warranty.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt 1.6 HDi engine prices for your Berlingo or C4

problems.h4: 2.0 BlueHDi Cam Chain Failure – The AdBlue Era Disaster
problems.affectedModels: Citroen Berlingo, C4 Grand Picasso, Dispatch, Relay, Spacetourer (2017–2023, DW10FCC (2.0 BlueHDi 150/180))
problems.rootCause: The camshaft drive chain stretches prematurely due to inadequate tensioning and chain quality. When the chain snaps or jumps timing, pistons strike valves, destroying the cylinder head. This prompted a major recall in 2025 .
problems.recommendation: This is a known, recall-worthy fault . Check if your VIN is covered for free repair first. If not, and you hear a rattle on cold start or have seen fault codes, replace the chain immediately at a specialist. If the engine has already failed, do not pay dealer prices for a new engine – we have specialist suppliers who can supply and fit a rebuilt BlueHDi engine with uprated timing components for half the cost.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt 2.0 BlueHDi engines with uprated timing chains

problems.h4: DV6 / 1.6 HDi Turbo & Injector Failure – Soot Is The Enemy
problems.affectedModels: Citroen Berlingo, C3, C4, C5 (2008–2016, DV6C, DV6D engines)
problems.rootCause: Leaking Bosch injector seals allow combustion soot to contaminate the engine oil. This soot blocks the turbo oil feed strainer, starving the turbo of oil, leading to turbo bearing failure (P0234 overboost code) .
problems.recommendation: Early warning signs include excessive oil consumption (from turbo seals) and a loss of power uphill. If you act when you first notice smoke or a check engine light, a turbo replacement with injector seals at a specialist is viable. If the turbo has fully failed (whining noise, loss of all power), metal has entered the oil system – fit a rebuilt engine from our marketplace rather than chasing secondary failures.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt 1.6 HDi engine and turbo package prices

problems.h4: Berlingo 1.6 HDi Oil Starvation – Piston & Ring Land Failure
problems.rootCause: A combination of soot contamination (from injector seals) and blocked oil pickups (from belt debris or carbon) causes oil pressure to drop. The result is piston ring land fracture, cylinder scoring, and complete loss of compression on one or more cylinders .
problems.recommendation: The Berlingo van is typically a working vehicle where downtime equals lost income. Do not attempt an in-place piston replacement – it’s a gamble that rarely pays off. Instead, use our marketplace to compare quotes for a ready-to-ship rebuilt Berlingo 1.6 HDi engine. Specialist suppliers keep these in stock and can often fit within two working days, making it the most cost-effective and time-efficient solution.
problems.cta: → Compare same-day fitment quotes for Berlingo 1.6 HDi rebuilt engines

problems.h4: C4 / C4 Picasso 1.6 THP Timing Chain & Carbon Build-Up
problems.affectedModels: Citroen C4, C4 Picasso, DS4, DS5 (2010–2016, EP6CDT / EP6DT (THP150, THP156, THP165))
problems.rootCause: The Prince engine (co-developed with BMW) suffers premature timing chain stretch due to inadequate tensioner design. Simultaneously, direct injection causes carbon build-up on intake valves, leading to cold start misfires, reduced power, and eventually valve damage.
problems.recommendation: If your C4 Picasso THP rattles for 1-2 seconds on cold startup, the chain tensioner has failed. Replace the chain kit immediately at a specialist independent, and while the intake manifold is off, pay for a walnut blast decarbonisation – it transforms the engine. If the engine has already failed, do not scrap the car; a rebuilt THP engine from our network costs a fraction of dealer new and fits in a weekend.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt THP engine prices for your C4 or DS4

finalCta.h4: Don’t Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Citroen
finalCta.paragraph: As you can see across 1.6 HDi, 2.0 BlueHDi, 1.2 PureTech, and 1.6 THP engines, Citroen models have clear, predictable failure points. In many cases, fitting a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is not only cheaper than chasing individual repairs at a dealer but also gives you a more reliable vehicle with a full warranty. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Citroen specialists – all of whom supply and fit rebuilt engines designed to eliminate the factory weaknesses above. Stop paying diagnostic fees for endless repairs. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.

engineSizes.h2: Citroen Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Citroen engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Citroen 1.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.4L HDi diesel was Citroen’s entry-level oil-burner throughout the 2000s, fitted primarily to the C2, C3, and Berlingo ranges. Based on the DV4 engine family, this 8-valve unit is generally robust but suffers from injector seal leaks and turbocharger failure on higher-mileage examples, particularly when service intervals have been extended beyond the recommended 12,000 miles. Engine code(s): DV4TD, 8HR, 8HT Compatible models (UK): C2, C3, C3 Picasso, Berlingo (2003–2010) Production years: 2003–2010 Common failure points: Injector seal leaks causing oil contamination; turbocharger failure from oil starvation
items.cta: Get quotes for Citroen 1.4L diesel engines

items.title: Citroen 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L BlueHDi (DV5) replaced the 1.6 HDi from 2018 onwards, bringing improved fuel economy and lower CO2 emissions through a selective catalytic reduction (SCR) AdBlue system. While generally reliable, these engines are not immune to EGR valve clogging on vehicles used predominantly for urban driving, and AdBlue system faults (P20E8, P20EE) are increasingly common as the cars age into the 80,000–120,000 mile bracket. Engine code(s): DV5, DV5RD, YH01 Compatible models (UK): C3, C3 Aircross, C4 Cactus, Berlingo (2018–present) Production years: 2018–present Common failure points: AdBlue system crystallisation (NOx sensor failures); EGR valve clogging on short journeys
items.cta: Get quotes for Citroen 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Citroen 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L HDi (DV6 / DV6TED4) is arguably the most common diesel engine ever fitted to a Citroen, appearing across virtually every model from 2005 to the late 2010s. The DV6 engine is known for excellent fuel economy (55–65 mpg) but has well-documented weaknesses: injector seal leaks that soot-contaminate engine oil, blocked turbo oil feed strainers leading to turbo failure (P0234), and EGR/DPF clogging on cars used for short trips. When these failures cascade, complete engine replacement is often more cost-effective than repair. Engine code(s): DV6A, DV6ATED4, DV6B, DV6C, DV6CTED, DV6D, DV6DBM, DV6DTED, DV6DTEDM, DV6FC, DV6FCTED, DV6FD, DV6FDTED, DV6UC, DV6UTED4, DLD-416, 9HD, 9HJ, BHY Compatible models (UK): Berlingo, C3, C3 Picasso, C4, C4 Grand Picasso, C4 Picasso, C5, Dispatch, DS3, DS4 (2005–2018) Production years: 2005–2018 Common failure points: Injector seal leaks causing soot in oil; turbo oil feed strainer blockage; EGR/DPF clogging; piston ring land fracture (Berlingo)
items.cta: Get quotes for Citroen 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Citroen 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The legendary XUD9 engine powered Citroen through the 1990s and early 2000s, long before HDi common-rail systems arrived. This indirect injection, mechanical diesel is famously robust, but common-rail conversions are impossible. The primary failure modes today are cylinder head cracking between the valves (a known XUD9 weakness) and Bosch injection pump seal failure. Given the age of these engines, finding a replacement unit is increasingly specialist work. Engine code(s): XUD9, XUD9A, XUD9BTF, XUD9T, XUD9TE, XUD9Y, TGD Compatible models (UK): ZX, Xantia, Saxo, Berlingo, Xsara, C15 van (1993–2002) Production years: 1993–2002 Common failure points: Cylinder head cracking between valves; injection pump seal failure; cambelt tensioner failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Citroen 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Citroen 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L diesel range spans three generations: the XUD11/B (1990s mechanical injection), the DW10 (HDi common-rail), and the DW10FC (BlueHDi AdBlue). Across all generations, replacement demand is driven by turbocharger failure (particularly on DW10 engines with variable geometry turbos) and, on BlueHDi units, cam chain failure that can write off the cylinder head. The DW10C and DW10CTED4 variants produce up to 163bhp and are common in larger cars and vans . Engine code(s): DW10, DW10ATED, DW10B, DW10BTED4, DW10C, DW10CTED4, DW10D, DW10FC, DW10FD, DW10TD, DW10TED4, DW10UTED4, XUD11, XUD11ATE, XUD11BTE, 4H03, 4HU, 4HV Compatible models (UK): C4, C4 Picasso, C5, C8, Dispatch, Relay, Xantia, Xsara, ZX (1994–2023) Production years: 1994–2023 Common failure points: Variable geometry turbo failure (DW10); cam chain stretch (DW10FC BlueHDi); EGR cooler failure (2.0 BlueHDi); crankshaft pulley failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Citroen 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Citroen 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2L HDi (DW12) was Citroen’s flagship diesel for the C5, C6, and large vans like the Relay and Dispatch. It offers strong torque (up to 400Nm) but is more complex than the 2.0L unit, with a dual-mass flywheel (DMF) that commonly fails between 80,000–120,000 miles and injector seal leaks that are labour-intensive to replace due to the engine’s transverse mounting in the C5. Engine code(s): DW12, DW12BTED4, DW12C, DW12CTED4, DW12TED4, 4H03 (high-power variant) Compatible models (UK): C5, C6, C8, Dispatch, Relay, Jumper (2006–2016) Production years: 2006–2016 Common failure points: Dual-mass flywheel failure; injector seal leaks (labour-intensive repair); EGR valve clogging
items.cta: Get quotes for Citroen 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Citroen 2.5L & 2.8L Diesel Engines (Sofim)
items.description: Citroen’s Relay and Jumper vans used Sofim-sourced 2.5L and 2.8L diesel engines (8140 series) from the 1990s through the early 2000s before transitioning to the PSA DW family. These Italian-built four-cylinder units are mechanically simple but suffer from injector pump failures (Bosch VP37) and cylinder head gasket issues. Replacement engines remain available through commercial vehicle specialists, though many have now covered well over 200,000 miles. Engine code(s): Sofim 8140.27 (2.5L), Sofim 8144.07 (2.5L), 8140.43S (2.8L) Compatible models (UK): Relay, Jumper (1994–2006) Production years: 1994–2006 Common failure points: Bosch VP37 injection pump failure; cylinder head gasket failure; injector seal leaks
items.cta: Get quotes for Citroen 2.5L and 2.8L diesel engines

items.title: Citroen 2.7L & 3.0L Diesel Engines (V6)
items.description: The DT17 and DT20 V6 diesel engines (Ford/PSA collaboration, also known as the “Ford Lion” engine) were fitted exclusively to the flagship C6 and third-generation C5. The 2.7L was available from 2005–2009, replaced by the 3.0L from 2009–2012. These engines are smooth and torquey but have developed a reputation for crankshaft bearing failure at higher mileages (100,000+), a catastrophic failure that typically requires complete engine replacement. Labour costs are high due to the engine’s tight packaging in the C6. Engine code(s): DT17ED4 (2.7L), DT17TED4 (2.7L), DT20C (3.0L) Compatible models (UK): C5 (2008–2012), C6 (2005–2012) Production years: 2005–2012 Common failure points: Crankshaft bearing failure (2.7L and 3.0L); DPF clogging; EGR valve failure; turbocharger failure (2.7L)
items.cta: Get quotes for Citroen 2.7L and 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Citroen 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0L petrol engine appears in Citroen’s city cars, primarily the C1 (a joint venture with Toyota and Peugeot). This three-cylinder unit is simple, chain-driven, and generally reliable, but it does suffer from coil pack failures (P0300–P0303) and, on earlier examples, valve clearance issues that can lead to poor cold-start performance. Replacement engines are inexpensive and widely available from breakers. Engine code(s): 1KR-FE (Toyota-sourced), CF10 Compatible models (UK): C1 (2005–2014) Production years: 2005–2014 Common failure points: Coil pack failure (cylinder misfire); valve clearance issues (cold start problems); exhaust corrosion
items.cta: Compare Citroen 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Citroen 1.1L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.1L TU1 engine (also known as the “80,000-mile engine” for its timing belt interval) powered the Saxo, C2, and C3 through the late 1990s and 2000s. This 8-valve unit is about as simple as a modern petrol engine gets — cast iron block, single overhead cam, mechanical throttle on early cars. Replacements are sought for two reasons: timing belt failure (interference engine, bent valves) or head gasket failure, which is common on TU engines beyond 80,000 miles. Engine code(s): TU1, TU1JP, HFX (1.1i) Compatible models (UK): Saxo, C2, C3, Xsara (1996–2008) Production years: 1996–2008 Common failure points: Timing belt failure (interference engine); cylinder head gasket failure; ignition coil failure
items.cta: Compare Citroen 1.1L petrol engine prices

items.title: Citroen 1.2L PureTech Engines
items.description: The 1.2L PureTech (EB2 series) replaced the naturally aspirated 1.4L and 1.6L petrol engines from 2014 onwards, becoming Citroen’s primary small petrol engine. The three-cylinder unit is characterful and economical but has a critical design flaw: the wet timing belt runs inside the engine oil, degrading over time and blocking the oil pickup strainer. If you hear a rattling cold start or see an oil pressure warning, the belt is failing. Replacement engines are in high demand, with many specialists now stocking rebuilt units featuring upgraded belt materials . Engine code(s): EB2, EB2DT, EB2DTS, HNZ Compatible models (UK): C3, C3 Aircross, C4, C4 Cactus, DS3, DS4 (2014–present) Production years: 2014–present Common failure points: Wet timing belt degradation (oil contamination, blocked strainer); oil pressure loss; turbocharger failure (EB2DT)
items.cta: Compare Citroen 1.2L PureTech engine prices

items.title: Citroen 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L TU3 and ET3 engines served Citroen for nearly 20 years across the Saxo, C2, C3, C4, and Berlingo. The TU3 is a robust, simple engine, but the later ET3 (16-valve, variable valve timing) introduced complexity without reliability gains. Head gasket failure is the most common terminal event across both families, typically occurring between 80,000–120,000 miles. The 1.4L is often replaced rather than repaired because complete running engines are readily available for £300–£600 from breakers. Engine code(s): TU3, TU3JP, TU3A, ET3, ET3J4, KFV (1.4i 16v), KFX (1.4i) Compatible models (UK): Saxo, C2, C3, C3 Picasso, C4, Berlingo, Xsara (1996–2012) Production years: 1996–2012 Common failure points: Cylinder head gasket failure; ignition coil failure (16-valve); lambda sensor failure
items.cta: Compare Citroen 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Citroen 1.6L Petrol Engines (Naturally Aspirated)
items.description: The 1.6L TU5 (also coded NFU) is one of Citroen’s most reliable petrol engines, fitted to the C2, C3, C4, Xsara, and Berlingo. This 16-valve unit produces 110bhp and, crucially, uses a conventional dry timing belt (not a wet belt like the PureTech). When they fail, it is almost always due to owner neglect — missed timing belt changes (every 80,000 miles or 10 years) or overheating from coolant loss. The engine is non-interference on most variants, meaning a snapped belt does not always bend valves. Engine code(s): TU5JP4, NFU Compatible models (UK): C2 VTS, C3 (2002–2009), C4 (2004–2010), Xsara, Berlingo (2000–2008) Production years: 2000–2010 Common failure points: Timing belt failure (neglect); coil pack failure; coolant loss leading to overheating
items.cta: Compare Citroen 1.6L naturally aspirated petrol engine prices

items.title: Citroen 1.6L Petrol Engines (Turbo – THP/Prince)
items.description: The 1.6L THP (EP6) engine, co-developed with BMW and known as the “Prince” engine, is a very different proposition from the naturally aspirated TU5. Fitted to sporty Citroens (DS3 Racing, DS4, C4 VTS), this direct-injection turbo engine has a reputation for timing chain stretch (rattle on cold start, P0011/P0012 codes), carbon build-up on intake valves (requiring walnut blasting every 40,000–60,000 miles), and high-pressure fuel pump failure . When the chain jumps timing or the pump fails catastrophically, a rebuilt engine often makes more sense than repair. Engine code(s): EP6, EP6CDT, EP6DT, 5FS Compatible models (UK): C4 (THP155, THP165), DS3 (THP155, THP165), DS4 (THP155, THP165), DS5 (2010–2018) Production years: 2007–2018 Common failure points: Timing chain stretch; intake valve carbon build-up (direct injection); high-pressure fuel pump failure; thermostat housing leak
items.cta: Compare Citroen 1.6L THP turbo petrol engine prices

items.title: Citroen 1.8L & 1.9L Petrol Engines (XU)
items.description: The 1.8L XU7 (XU7JP) and 1.9L XU9 (XU9J2) engines powered the Xantia, Xsara, and ZX through the 1990s. These cast-iron blocks are heavy but nearly indestructible. The primary failure today is not the engine itself but the Bosch Motronic ECU or engine management wiring, particularly on cars that have sat unused. Replacement engines are usually sourced from breakers, with specialists performing ECU swaps to match immobiliser codes. Engine code(s): XU7JP (1.8L 16v), XU9J2 (1.9L 8v), XU9J4/Z (1.9L 16v – GTi) Compatible models (UK): ZX, Xantia, Xsara, Berlingo (1993–2000) Production years: 1993–2000 Common failure points: ECU/wiring harness failure (age-related); distributor failure; coolant temperature sensor failure
items.cta: Compare Citroen 1.8L and 1.9L petrol engine prices

items.title: Citroen 2.0L Petrol Engines (EW / XU10)
items.description: The 2.0L EW10 (also coded RFJ) is the most common larger petrol engine in Citroen’s 2000s lineup, fitted to the C4, C5, Xsara, and Peugeot 307 (shared platform). The 16-valve EW10 produces 138–143bhp and uses a timing belt, not a chain. Replacement demand is driven by coil pack failure (P0300), lambda sensor failure, and — on higher-mileage examples — oil consumption from worn valve stem seals. The earlier XU10 (1990s) is similar but uses a distributor ignition system. Engine code(s): EW10J4, EW10A, RFJ, RFN, XU10J2, XU10J4 Compatible models (UK): C4, C5, Xsara, Xantia, C8 (2000–2010) Production years: 2000–2010 Common failure points: Coil pack failure; valve stem seal wear (oil consumption); lambda sensor failure; catalyst failure
items.cta: Compare Citroen 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Citroen 2.5L Petrol Engines (CX – Classic)
items.description: The 2.5L petrol engine (EN5L) is a classic Citroen unit from the CX era — a four-cylinder, carburetted or fuel-injected engine produced in the 1980s. These are rarely replaced today except by classic car restorers, as CX values have risen significantly. The engine suffers from carburettor icing (on carb models), camshaft wear, and cooling system corrosion if the incorrect coolant has been used over the decades . Engine code(s): EN5L Compatible models (UK): CX (1984–1990) Production years: 1984–1990 Common failure points: Carburettor icing (carb models); camshaft wear; cooling system corrosion; fuel system perishing (ethanol-blended fuels)
items.cta: Enquire about Citroen 2.5L classic petrol engine availability

items.title: Citroen ë-C4 / ë-Berlingo Electric Motor
items.description: Motor code(s): Nidec-PSA M3 (100kW/136bhp), M4 (115kW/156bhp – ë-C4 X) Compatible models (UK): ë-C4, ë-C4 X, ë-Berlingo (2021–present) Production years: 2021–present Important notes: Failure is rare (typically bearing noise or inverter electronics). Exchange units available from specialist EV breakers. Always verify battery health before committing to motor repair.
items.cta: Enquire about Citroen electric motor repair and exchange

items.title: Citroen ë-Dispatch / ë-Spacetourer Electric Motor
items.description: Motor code(s): Stellantis E-Motors (100kW/136bhp) Compatible models (UK): ë-Dispatch, ë-Spacetourer (2021–present) Production years: 2021–present Important notes: Shared with Peugeot e-Expert and Toyota Proace Electric. Motor is largely reliable; most EV powertrain issues relate to the battery management system (BMS), not the motor itself.

engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Citroen engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Citroen specialists.

entries.title: EB2 (HMZ) 1.2L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Found in Citroen C3 (2016+), C4 Cactus, and C4 Picasso. Produces 82hp. The wet timing belt degrades inside the engine, shedding rubber debris that blocks the oil strainer. Oil pressure drops, starving the turbo and crankshaft bearings. A quality rebuilt unit includes the latest revised belt compound, new oil pump, and a full sump and gallery clean.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2 engine replacement for your C3 or C4

entries.title: EB2DT (HNZ) 1.2L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Used across C3, C4, Berlingo, and C4 Picasso from 2014 onward with 110hp. Same wet belt design as the EB2, but higher output means turbo failure is more common when belt debris restricts oil flow. Rebuilt engines feature upgraded belt materials, new oil strainer, and often a replacement turbocharger to guarantee longevity.
entries.cta: Compare EB2DT PureTech engine prices with upgraded belt kit

entries.title: DV6TED4 (9HZ) 1.6L Diesel
entries.description: Engine The 90hp version powers C3, C4, Berlingo, and C4 Picasso (2005–2015). The EGR cooler cracks internally, mixing coolant with exhaust soot; the DPF clogs; and the turbo vanes jam from carbon buildup. A specialist rebuilt unit includes an upgraded EGR cooler assembly, full DPF clean or replacement, and turbo inspection.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 9HZ DV6 engine replacement with cleaned EGR system

entries.title: DV6C (9HZ) 1.6L Diesel
entries.description: Engine Produces 110–115hp in C4, C4 Picasso, DS3, and DS4 (2010–2018). The known failure path: EGR cooler cracking
entries.cta: Compare DV6C 1.6 HDi engine prices for C4 and DS3 models

entries.title: DV6DTED (9HR) 1.6L Diesel
entries.description: Engine 112hp version fitted to C4, DS4, DS5, and Berlingo (2011–2018). Suffers identical EGR cooler and DPF issues as other DV6 engines, plus premature timing belt failure on higher-mileage examples. Rebuilt units include a new timing belt, water pump, EGR cooler, and full DPF regeneration.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt 9HR 1.6 HDi engines for DS4 and Berlingo

entries.title: DV4TED4 (8HX) 1.4L Diesel
entries.description: Engine 70hp unit used in C2, C3, and C3 Picasso (2005–2010). Older, simpler design than the DV6 but suffers injector seal leaks, EGR valve clogging, and occasional turbo wear. Less catastrophic than DV6 failures but still drives replacement on high-mileage city cars. Rebuilt engines include new injector seals, cleaned EGR valve, and turbo inspection.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 8HX 1.4 HDi engine replacement

entries.title: DV6FE (BHZ) 1.6L Diesel
entries.description: Engine 100hp BlueHDi unit in C3, C4, C4 Picasso, and Berlingo (2014–present). The critical failure is AdBlue crystallisation inside the tank, heater, and injector lines. The ECU locks out engine starts after a countdown. Rebuilt engines include a new AdBlue tank assembly (with integrated heater/sender), cleaned NOx sensors, and software reset.
entries.cta: Compare BHZ 1.6 BlueHDi engine prices with new AdBlue system

entries.title: DV6FCE 1.6L Diesel
entries.description: Engine 120hp version fitted to C4, DS4, DS5, and Jumpy (2015–present). Same AdBlue crystallisation problem plus DPF clogging on short journeys. Rebuilt units include a replacement AdBlue tank, new DPF, and EGR system clean.
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6FCE BlueHDi engine replacement for DS5 and Jumpy

entries.title: DW10FE (AHL) 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Engine 150hp unit found in C5, C5 Aircross, Dispatch, and Jumper (2014–present). AdBlue system failure is the dominant replacement driver, with crystallisation blocking the tank injector. Turbo actuator failure also occurs. A quality rebuilt engine includes a new AdBlue tank, injector, DPF, and turbo actuator.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt AHL 2.0 BlueHDi engines for C5 and Dispatch

entries.title: DW10FD 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Engine 180hp version in C5 Aircross, DS5, and SpaceTourer (2016–present). Same AdBlue crystallisation issues as other BlueHDi engines, but the higher output means turbo failure is more common when oil contamination occurs. Rebuilt units include a new turbocharger, AdBlue tank assembly, and full oil system flush.
entries.cta: Compare DW10FD 2.0 BlueHDi engine replacement costs

entries.title: EP6DT (5FX) 1.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine 150hp version in C4, DS3, and DS4 (2007–2015). High-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) failure is the most common catastrophic fault — the pump loses pressure, the engine cranks but won't start. Timing chain tensioner wear also causes rattle and potential jump. Rebuilt units include a new HPFP and revised timing chain kit with upgraded tensioner.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 5FX 1.6 THP engine replacement

entries.title: EP6DTS (5FY) 1.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine 175hp tuned version in DS3 Racing and DS4 (2010–2015). HPFP failure occurs earlier than on lower-output versions due to higher fuel pressure demands. Timing chain stretch is also common. Rebuilt units feature an uprated HPFP, heavy-duty timing chain kit, and carbon-cleaned intake valves.
entries.cta: Compare 5FY 1.6 THP engine prices for DS3 Racing

entries.title: EP6C 1.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine 120hp version in C4, DS3, and DS4 (2010–2018). HPFP failure and timing chain wear are the primary failure modes. Rebuilt units include a new HPFP, revised timing chain tensioner, and cleaned fuel injectors.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt EP6C 1.6 petrol engines for C4 and DS3

entries.title: DW10BTED4 (RHR) 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Engine 136hp in C5, C8, Xsara, and late Xantia (2000–2008). Injector seal leaks and high-pressure fuel pump failure are the main replacement drivers. The pump is no longer manufactured, making rebuilt units the only viable option. Quality rebuilds include a refurbished HPFP with upgraded seals and new injector washers.
entries.cta: Get quotes for RHR 2.0 HDi engine replacement

entries.title: DW10A (RHZ) 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Engine 110hp unit in Xantia, Xsara, and early C5 (1999–2005). Injection pump failure and head gasket leaks are common at high mileage. Rebuilt engines include a reconditioned HPFP, new head gasket, and timing belt kit.
entries.cta: Compare RHZ 2.0 HDi engine prices for Xantia and Xsara

entries.title: TU5JP (NFZ) 1.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine 110hp engine in Saxo, Xsara, Xsara Picasso (1998–2005). Simple, robust, but suffers head gasket failure and coil pack failure after 100,000+ miles. No complex electronics — a rebuilt unit is straightforward and cost-effective. Includes new head gasket, valve stem seals, and timing belt.
entries.cta: Get quotes for NFZ 1.6 petrol engine for Saxo and Xsara

entries.title: TU3M (KFW) 1.4L Petrol
entries.description: Engine 75hp unit in Saxo, C2, Xsara (1997–2005). Timing belt failure is the primary catastrophic event. The engine is non-interference on some variants, but belt snap still causes towing costs and risk of valve damage. Rebuilt units include a new timing belt kit, water pump, and head gasket. engineYears.h2: Citroën Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major Citroën engine changes across the full UK model range - from the XU petrol and XUD diesel era of the early 1990s through to today's BlueHDi, PureTech and all-electric platforms - organised so owners can identify their exact engine by vehicle year and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: XU Petrol & XUD Turbodiesel Generation 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The early 1990s marked Citroën's high point for the XU petrol family and the XUD turbodiesel - both PSA-developed workhorses that powered the Xantia, ZX and BX, delivering strong real-world durability that still defines these cars' reputation in the UK classic and high-mileage market today. Key Changes PSA XU petrol family (1.6, 1.8, 2.0) in 8v and 16v variants powers the Xantia from launch (1993) and continues across ZX and BX runout XUD 1.9 turbodiesel - indirect injection, pre-common-rail - established as Citroën's benchmark diesel; 1.9 TD and 1.9 SD variants sold widely in UK 2.1 TD five-cylinder diesel introduced in Xantia as the flagship diesel, sharing architecture with the XUD family Citroën introduced 3.0 V6 in Xantia from 1997 - the PRV-derived unit powering the range-topper Euro 1 and early Euro 2 regulations drive incremental catalyst fitment updates through the period Main Engines XU5 (TU5) - 1.6 petrol 8v/16v (ZX, Xantia, Xsara early) XU7JP / XU7JP4 - 1.8 petrol 8v/16v (Xantia, ZX, Xsara) XU10J2C / XU10J4R - 2.0 petrol 8v/16v (Xantia, ZX) XUD9 / XUD9TE - 1.9 diesel naturally aspirated/turbocharged (Xantia, ZX, BX) D8A - 2.1 TD five-cylinder diesel (Xantia TD) Popular Models Citroën Xantia Citroën Xantia Estate (Break) Citroën ZX Citroën BX (runout) Known For XUD 1.9 turbodiesel widely regarded as one of PSA's finest engines - mechanically simple, pre-common-rail robustness, widely rebuilt to 200,000+ miles XU petrol 16v in 2.0 form delivers a lively, naturally aspirated character and straightforward maintenance; timing belt adherence essential beyond 70,000 miles XUD indirect injection produces modest power by modern standards but exceptional longevity - a favourite among long-distance owners 2.1 TD five-cylinder less common and harder to source rebuilt; cylinder head gasket failure is a known high-mileage concern [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: XU5J, XU7JP, XU7JP4, XU10J2C, XU10J4R, XUD9, XUD9TE, XUD11BTE, D8A
years.cta: Get quotes for 1992–1997 Citroën replacement engines - XU and XUD specialists available across UK

years.preview: HDi Common Rail Revolution & Xsara Era 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The introduction of the HDi direct-injection common-rail diesel in 1998 - the world's second production common-rail passenger-car diesel, pioneered alongside Alfa Romeo and Bosch - transformed Citroën's diesel reputation and immediately dominated UK sales through the Xantia, Xsara and C5. Key Changes PSA HDi (Haut rendement Diesel injection) common-rail system launched in Xantia and Xsara (1999 UK models, 1998 introduction) 2.0 HDi 90hp and 109hp variants introduced as the core diesel offering across the Xsara and Xantia range XU petrol family continues across Xsara range; 1.4, 1.6, 1.8, 2.0 petrol lineup remains Citroën C5 announced (2000 UK launch) with EW-series petrol and DW-series HDi diesel Euro 3 compliance from 2000 drives catalyst and ECU updates across all petrol variants XUD turbodiesel phased out - HDi directly replaces it from 1999 model year Main Engines DW8 (WJZ / WJY) - 1.9 HDi - first common-rail diesel (Xsara, Berlingo early) DW10 (RHY/RHS) - 2.0 HDi 90/110hp - common-rail diesel (Xantia, Xsara, C5 pre-launch) XU7JP4 - 1.8 16v petrol (Xsara, Berlingo petrol) XU10J4R - 2.0 16v petrol (Xsara VTS, Xantia 16v) Popular Models Citroën Xsara Citroën Xsara Picasso Citroën Xantia Citroën Berlingo (Mk1) Known For 2.0 HDi a step-change over the XUD in throttle response and refinement; owners praise fuel economy of 50+ mpg in regular use DW8 1.9 HDi identified as slightly less refined than the DW10; injector seals can weep on higher-mileage examples beyond 90,000 miles Xsara Picasso becomes one of Citroën's best-selling UK models - HDi-powered examples generate strong long-term ownership loyalty XU petrol family showing age by late 1990s versus competitors but remains mechanically dependable with regular belt changes [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: DW8, WJZ, WJY, DW10, RHY, RHS, XU7JP4, XU10J4R, TU3JP
years.cta: Compare 1997–2001 Citroën HDi diesel engine prices from UK-verified rebuilders

years.preview: C5 / C3 Launch, EW Petrol & DW10 Diesel Expansion 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The launch of the C5 in 2001 introduced the modern EW petrol family and the DW10 HDi diesel in a fully integrated PSA platform, while the C3 in 2002 brought the TU and DV4 engine ranges to a new-generation supermini - establishing the HDi brand name firmly in the UK consciousness. Key Changes EW-series petrol family (EW6, EW7, EW10) introduced on C5 (2001) - DOHC, 16v, replacing older XU units C3 (2002) launches with TU-series petrol (1.1, 1.4, 1.6) and DV4 1.4 HDi diesel - the first DV4 diesel in a Citroën supermini DW10 2.0 HDi continues as the volume diesel across C5, Xsara Picasso and C8 MPV 1.6 HDi DV6 family first appearance (2003–2004) in C3, C2 and Berlingo 2.2 HDi (DW12) introduced on C5 and C8 for higher-output diesel requirement Euro 3 compliance firm across all new introductions; catalyst/EGR combination standard Main Engines EW6 (NFU/TU5JP4) - 1.6 16v petrol (C3, C5 entry) EW7J4 (LFY) - 1.8 16v petrol (C5, Xsara) EW10J4 (RFJ/RFN) - 2.0 16v petrol (C5, Xsara VTS, C8) DV4TD - 1.4 HDi 8v diesel (C3, C2, Berlingo) DV6TED4 (9HX/9HZ) - 1.6 HDi 16v diesel (C3, Berlingo, C4 early) DW10 (RHY/RHS/RHR) - 2.0 HDi diesel (C5, Xsara Picasso, C8) DW12 - 2.2 HDi diesel (C5, C8) Popular Models Citroën C5 Citroën C3 (Mk1) Citroën Xsara Picasso Citroën Berlingo Citroën C8 Known For EW10 2.0 petrol a competent naturally aspirated unit - Euro 3 compliant, smooth, but lacks the character of the old 16v XU; EW-family timing belt intervals critical at 80,000 miles DV6 1.6 HDi becomes one of PSA's most widely deployed diesels - robust under regular maintenance, though EGR valve fouling a common 60,000-mile service item DW10 2.0 HDi proves a reliable fleet workhorse; high-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) wear on early injection pumps a known issue on older examples C5's Hydractive III suspension complexity draws attention away from its excellent engine range - the EW petrol and DW10 diesel are notably underrated [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: NFU, TU5JP4, EW7J4, EW10J4, RFJ, RFN, DV4TD, DV4TED, DV6TED4, 9HX, 9HZ, RHY, RHS, RHR, DW12
years.cta: Find rebuilt 2001–2005 Citroën engines - C5, C3, Xsara Picasso and Berlingo units in stock

years.preview: C4 Launch, 1.6 THP & Euro 4 DV6 Peak 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The C4 (2004) and its new 1.6 EP6 THP turbocharged petrol - PSA's first production turbopetrol across the mainstream range - alongside Euro 4 updates to the DV6 HDi diesel family marked the transition from naturally aspirated petrol dominance to turbo-first thinking. Key Changes Citroën C4 launched (2004) with TU, EW and DV6 HDi engines; 1.6 THP EP6 turbocharged petrol debuts from 2006 1.6 EP6 THP (Prince engine, jointly developed with BMW) introduced on C4 and C5 from 2006 - 120hp and 150hp variants DV6 HDi 1.6 Euro 4 upgrades across C3, C4, Berlingo, and C5 lineup C5 Mk2 facelift (2008) brings updated EW10 petrol and DW10 HDi with Euro 4 DPF fitment 2.7 HDi V6 diesel introduced on C6 flagship (2005) - PSA's most powerful diesel to date Main Engines EP6 (5FW/5FX) - 1.6 THP - turbocharged petrol 120/150hp (C4, C5, C4 Picasso) EW10J4 (RFJ/RFN) - 2.0 16v petrol (C5 Mk2, C4 Coupé) DV6TED4 (9HX/9HZ/9HY) - 1.6 HDi Euro 4 (C3, C4, Berlingo, Xsara Picasso) DW10 (RHR/RHS) - 2.0 HDi Euro 4 (C5, C4 Picasso, C8) DT17 - 2.7 HDi V6 diesel (C6) Popular Models Citroën C4 (Mk1) Citroën C5 (Mk2 early) Citroën C4 Picasso / Grand Picasso Citroën Berlingo Mk2 Citroën C6 Known For 1.6 EP6 THP praised for performance and economy but develops a documented timing chain wear fault on pre-2011 build examples - chain tensioner hydraulic pressure failure reported from 60,000 miles DV6 HDi 1.6 in Euro 4 form remains highly reliable in UK fleet use; EGR blanking a popular owner modification to extend service life 2.7 HDi V6 in the C6 is a prestige diesel unit - smooth and powerful but complex twin-turbo arrangement leads to expensive repairs on neglected examples EP6 THP in C4 earns a strong performance reputation tempered by early chain stretch - a known issue Citroën later addressed via revised chain tensioners [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: 5FW, 5FX, EP6, EW10J4, RFJ, RFN, DV6TED4, 9HX, 9HZ, 9HY, RHR, RHS, DT17
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004–2008 Citroën C4, C5 and Berlingo replacement engines - THP and HDi specialists

years.preview: C3 Picasso, DS Line & Euro 5 Transition 🔴 Major Reliability Revision
years.description: Euro 5 compliance from 2009 drove significant hardware changes across the DV6 and DW10 diesel families - particularly revised EGR systems, DPF fitment as standard, and updated engine management - while the C3 Picasso and early DS-branded models began carrying the revised EP6 petrol with improved timing chain hardware. Key Changes Euro 5 compliance mandates DPF as standard across all new Citroën diesel models from September 2009 DV6 (1.6 HDi) family updated to Euro 5 spec: revised EGR cooler, DPF, and new ECU calibration - codes move from 9HX/9HZ to 9HC/9HF DW10 (2.0 HDi) updated to Euro 5: DPF integration, particulate filter management via fuel injection strategy EP6 timing chain tensioner revised on 2010+ build C4 and C4 Picasso examples - part of a rolling production fix DS brand separates from Citroën from 2009 (DS3 in 2010, DS4 in 2011) carrying same engine range as C-series models C3 Picasso and C4 Picasso become high-volume UK fleet sellers; DV6 and THP the dominant engines Main Engines EP6C (5F01/5F02) - 1.6 THP - revised chain-tensioner turbopetrol (C4, DS3, DS4, C4 Picasso) TU5JP4 (NFU) - 1.6 16v naturally aspirated petrol (C3 Picasso, Berlingo) DV6C (9HC/9HF) - 1.6 HDi Euro 5 - DPF (C3, C3 Picasso, Berlingo, C4, DS3) DW10C (RH02) - 2.0 HDi Euro 5 - DPF (C4 Picasso, C5, Grand Picasso) Popular Models Citroën C4 Picasso / Grand C4 Picasso Citroën C3 Picasso Citroën Berlingo Mk2 Citroën C5 (Mk2 late) Citroën DS3 Known For Revised EP6 tensioner from 2010 production is a significant improvement - post-2010 C4 and DS3 THP examples far safer to buy used than 2006–2009 build DV6C Euro 5 with DPF sees DPF regeneration issues in urban/stop-start driving - warning light faults and forced regeneration required every 300–400 miles for town-only use DW10 2.0 HDi in Grand C4 Picasso is a long-lived touring diesel; dual-mass flywheel wear reported on manual gearbox examples above 90,000 miles DS3 and C3 Picasso inherit a well-resolved DV6 - these are among the safest used Citroën diesel buys of the era [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: EP6C, 5F01, 5F02, NFU, TU5JP4, DV6C, 9HC, 9HF, DW10C, RH02
years.cta: Compare 2008–2012 Citroën diesel and THP replacement engine prices - Euro 5 units from UK suppliers

years.preview: PureTech 1.2 Introduction & First BlueHDi Diesel 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction 🔴 Technology Introduction
years.description: The arrival of the EB-series PureTech 1.2 three-cylinder turbocharged petrol in 2012 was Citroën's biggest powertrain shift in a decade - a new-generation, low-displacement downsized unit replacing the long-running TU and early EP6 naturally aspirated engines across the C-range - while the BlueHDi system brought AdBlue SCR diesel technology to the range from 2013. faq.h2: Citroen Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Citroen owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Citroen engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: For a rebuilt 1.6 HDi (DV6) fitted to a Berlingo or C4, expect £2,500–£4,500 supply and fit from a specialist — around half what a main dealer would charge for a new engine. A reconditioned 2.0 HDi (DW10) for a C5 or Dispatch typically runs £3,000–£5,000 fitted. The cheapest route is a used engine from a breaker at £800–£1,500, but you get no warranty and unknown history. For flagship options like the 2.7 V6 HDi (C6), rebuild costs hit £4,000–£6,500. Always compare at least three specialist quotes before committing.
items.cta: Compare Citroen engine replacement prices now

items.question: What affects the cost of a Citroen engine replacement?
items.answer: Four main factors: engine size and complexity (1.6 HDi costs less than a 2.7 V6); condition (rebuilt with warranty costs more than used "as-is" units); vehicle age (modern BlueHDi engines require more electronics calibration); and supply-only versus supply-and-fit (fitting adds £400–£800 depending on model). Location also matters — London and South East labour rates run 20–30% higher than the rest of the UK. Our comparison tool brings transparent pricing from verified specialists, so you can see exactly what each supplier includes in their quote.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Citroen engine specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Citroen?
items.answer: In most cases, yes — especially if the chassis, gearbox, and bodywork are solid. A rebuilt 1.6 HDi Berlingo engine costs £2,500–£4,000 fitted, while replacing the whole van would run £8,000–£15,000 for a comparable used example. Rebuilt Citroen engines also feature upgraded components addressing original failure points (revised timing chains, better piston rings, improved injector seals), often outlasting the original factory unit. If your Citroen is worth under £1,500, a used engine might make more sense. For any car worth £2,500 or more, replacement is nearly always the financially sound decision.
items.cta: Find reconditioned Citroen engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: What's included in a Citroen engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: A full supply-and-fit package includes sourcing a rebuilt or reconditioned engine, removing the failed unit, installing the replacement, new oil, filters, coolant, and a new cambelt or timing chain where applicable. You also get a workmanship warranty (typically matching the engine warranty), disposal of the old engine, and a road test to confirm everything operates correctly. Fitting alone typically costs £400–£800, depending on model complexity — transverse engines like the C4 take longer than longitudinal units in the C5. Always confirm whether ancillary items (water pump, injector seals, turbo oil line) are included in the quoted price.
items.cta: Compare Citroen engine supply and fit deals

items.question: How long does a Citroen engine replacement take?
items.answer: For common models like a Berlingo 1.6 HDi or C3 1.2 PureTech, expect 3–5 working days from booking to completion. More complex vehicles — C5 2.7 V6 or C6 — take 5–7 days due to tighter engine bay access and additional system calibrations. The biggest variable is engine availability: our network stocks commonly replaced codes (DV6, DW10, EB2 PureTech) for next-day dispatch, but rarer units (DV12, DT17 V6) may require 3–5 days lead time. Most specialists offer a courtesy car or loaner for the duration. Ask your chosen supplier for a firm timeline before authorising work.
items.cta: Use our free comparison tool to see real lead times from Citroen specialists

items.question: Do reconditioned Citroen engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes — every Citroen engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, with many rebuilders offering 24‑month extended cover for an additional cost. The warranty typically covers all major internal components: crankshaft, conrods, pistons, piston rings, cylinder head, valves, camshaft, timing chain or belt, oil pump, and water pump (where fitted). Exclusions usually apply to ancillary components (turbocharger, injectors, EGR valve) unless specifically listed as replaced. Always confirm exact warranty terms — what's covered, claim procedure, and labour contribution — directly with your chosen supplier before purchase.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Citroen specialist rebuilders with full warranty cover

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Citroen engine?
items.answer: Used engines cost less (£800–£1,500) and suit tight budgets or cars you plan to sell within 12 months. However, you inherit unknown mileage, service history gaps, and no warranty — a gamble on a 1.6 HDi with potential injector seal or turbo issues. A reconditioned or rebuilt engine (£2,500–£5,000 fitted) includes new piston rings, bearings, timing components, gaskets, a full clean, and a 12‑24 month warranty. For failure-prone Citroen engines like the 1.2 PureTech (wet belt) or 1.6 THP (timing chain), a reconditioned unit with upgraded parts is strongly recommended — the extra cost buys another 100,000+ miles of reliable service.

items.question: What are the most common Citroen engine failures?
items.answer: The 1.6 HDi (DV6) suffers injector seal leaks causing soot‑contaminated oil, blocked turbo strainers, and eventual turbo failure — symptoms include loss of power and excessive smoke. The 1.2 PureTech (EB2) has a wet timing belt that degrades, blocking the oil pickup and causing catastrophic oil starvation. The 1.6 THP (Prince engine) stretches its timing chain and builds carbon on intake valves, leading to cold start rattle and misfires. The 2.0 BlueHDi (DW10FC) can snap its cam chain, destroying the cylinder head. The 2.7/3.0 V6 HDi suffers crankshaft bearing failure beyond 100,000 miles. All these failures typically lead to replacement rather than repair once significant internal damage has occurred.

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Citroen engine?
items.answer: Always compare at least three quotes — prices on the same engine code (e.g., 1.6 HDi DV6) can vary by £1,000+ between suppliers. Consider a used engine (£800–£1,500) if on a tight budget or selling the car soon. Choose a reconditioned engine with upgraded components (£2,500–£5,000 fitted) if you plan to keep the Citroen for another 3–5 years. Ask for supply-only if you have a trusted local garage that will fit it for less than the specialist's fitting fee. Always confirm whether delivery, fitting, VAT, and ancillary parts (oil, filters, cambelt) are included in the quoted price. Use our tool to filter by warranty length, location, and customer rating to find the best value, not just the lowest headline number.
